mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS signals easier financial conditions, lower equity discount rates, and a positive S&P 500 return over the next month when policy is moving down from a restrictive stance.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls by at least 0.25 pp over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the signal's 3-year rolling rank correlation stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE increases recession odds and suppresses earnings expectations, so S&P 500 forward returns are negative over the next month when unemployment is trending up.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 pp over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the sign of the effect is unstable across two consecutive rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in RSAFS and GDPC1 signals broad final-demand strength, which supports revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if joint-high demand readings fail to improve 60-day forward SPX returns relative to non-joint periods, or if either proxy alone explains the effect without interaction gain.

mechanism_narrative: A falling INDPRO indicates industrial output contraction and weaker cyclical earnings, which should push S&P 500 returns lower over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if monthly INDPRO declines are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or if the signal loses sign consistency in rolling 2-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, real policy is loose relative to inflation and equities gain from a lower real discount rate, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds FEDFUNDS by at least 1.0 pp
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation/low-rate triggers do not outperform unconditional returns, or if positive SPX outcomes are absent in at least 60% of triggered samples.

mechanism_narrative: A stable PAYEMS series implies labor-market resilience and supports earnings visibility, creating a modest positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS is flat or rises over the prior 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if flat-to-rising PAYEMS periods do not produce above-baseline 5-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears after controlling for the prior month's market trend.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals housing-led demand weakness that usually transmits to cyclical earnings and lowers S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by at least 8% year-over-year
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST contractions are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the effect is absent outside one-off shock months.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling FEDFUNDS and subdued CPIAUCSL implies easing real borrowing costs, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month; the effect is strongest when both series move in the same direction.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines over 2 months and CPIAUCSL inflation is not accelerating
failure_condition: Reject if combined easing/disinflation triggers do not outperform single-series signals, or if triggered 20-day SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of cases.
